title: achillea ptarmica var. grandiflora
intro: Achillea ptarmica var. grandiflora, also known as the Yarrow 'Fern-Leaf', is a perennial herb belonging to the Asteraceae family, characterized by its fern-like leaves and clusters of small white flowers. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antispasmodic effects, making it effective in treating wounds, digestive issues, and menstrual cramps. The health benefits of Achillea ptarmica var. grandiflora include reducing fever, relieving pain, and improving digestion, as well as its use as a diuretic and expectorant. Herbal preparations of the plant include teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used to make topical creams, salves, and ointments, or consumed as a medicinal drink.
